name: Gmail send
description: The first outbox consumer (issue #306): watches email messages you released with social.send_message and stages each as an outbox artifact — recipient, subject, body, plus the exact Gmail API call. Nothing leaves until you approve the item (or a standing 'always allow' grant matches); the gateway executor performs the send through the pull.gmail connection's credential.
prompt: When I release an email draft for sending, stage it in the outbox as the rendered message so I can approve, edit, or discard it. Never call the network yourself — the outbox executor performs approved sends through my Gmail connection.

vault.why: Reads released email messages (thread, recipients, body text) and stages each as an outbox artifact for your decision. Holds no send capability of its own — external writes are the outbox executor's, behind your approval.
